relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus brings the inner three up the mountain.
His hidden glory shines through face and garments.
Moses and Elijah appear in conversation with him.
Peter wants to preserve the moment in tents.
The bright cloud interrupts: this beloved Son must be listened to.
The disciples fall in holy fear.
Jesus touches them and speaks fearlessness.
They lift their eyes and see Jesus alone.
The vision is held until resurrection.
The disciples ask about Elijah.
Elijah’s restoration role is affirmed.
Elijah has already come in the John-pattern and was not recognized.
They understand he speaks of John.
They descend from glory into human need.
A father asks mercy for his afflicted son.
The disciples could not heal him.
Jesus grieves the untrusting generation and calls the child near.
The troubling force leaves and the child is healed.
Privately the disciples ask why they could not release him.
Mustard-seed trust can move mountains.
A later manuscript note connects this kind with prayer and fasting.
Jesus again reveals the handing-over of the Son of Humanity.
Death and third-day rising bring grief before understanding.
The temple-tax question comes to Peter.
Jesus asks how kings treat sons and outsiders.
The sons are free.
Freedom chooses not to cause stumbling and provision appears from the sea.
